Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface StringVerifier

All Superinterfaces:
ExtensibleObjectVerifier<StringVerifier,​String>
All Known Implementing Classes:
StringVerifierImpl, StringVerifierNoOp
public interface StringVerifier
extends ExtensibleObjectVerifier<StringVerifier,​String>
Verifies the requirements of a String.

All methods (except for ExtensibleObjectVerifier.asString() and those found in ExtensibleObjectValidator) imply ExtensibleObjectVerifier.isNotNull().

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type Method Description
    InetAddressVerifier asInetAddress()
    Ensures that the actual value contains a valid Internet address format.
    StringVerifier asInetAddress​(Consumer<InetAddressVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    UriVerifier asUri()
    Returns a verifier for the URI representation of the value.
    StringVerifier asUri​(Consumer<UriVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    UrlVerifier asUrl()
    Returns a verifier for the URL representation of the value.
    StringVerifier asUrl​(Consumer<UrlVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier contains​(String expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
    StringVerifier doesNotContain​(String value)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
    StringVerifier doesNotEndWith​(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
    StringVerifier doesNotStartWith​(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
    StringVerifier endsWith​(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
    StringVerifier isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    StringVerifier isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    StringVerifier isTrimmed()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing whitespace.
    SizeVerifier length()
    Returns a verifier for the length of the actual value.
    StringVerifier length​(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
    Verifies nested requirements.
    StringVerifier startsWith​(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
    StringVerifier trim()
    Trims whitespace at the beginning and end of the actual value.

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension.ExtensibleObjectVerifier

    asString, asString, getActual, isActualAvailable, isEqualTo, isEqualTo, isInstanceOf, isNotEqualTo, isNotEqualTo, isNotInstanceOf, isNotNull, isNotOneOf, isNotSameObjectAs, isNull, isOneOf, isSameObjectAs

  • Method Details

    • startsWith

      StringVerifier startsWith​(String prefix)
      Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the value that the string must start with
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if prefix is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value does not start with prefix

    • doesNotStartWith

      StringVerifier doesNotStartWith​(String prefix)
      Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the value that the string may not start with
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if prefix is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value starts with prefix

    • endsWith

      StringVerifier endsWith​(String suffix)
      Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
      Parameters:
      suffix - the value that the string must end with
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if suffix is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value does not end with suffix

    • doesNotEndWith

      StringVerifier doesNotEndWith​(String suffix)
      Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
      Parameters:
      suffix - the value that the string may not end with
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if suffix is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value ends with suffix

    • contains

      StringVerifier contains​(String expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
      Parameters:
      expected - the value that the string must contain
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value does not contain expected

    • doesNotContain

      StringVerifier doesNotContain​(String value)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value that the string may not contain
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if value is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value contains value

    • isEmpty

      StringVerifier isEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value is not empty
      See Also:
      trim()

    • isNotEmpty

      StringVerifier isNotEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value is empty
      See Also:
      trim()

    • length

      SizeVerifier length()
      Returns a verifier for the length of the actual value.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the length of the actual value

    • length

      StringVerifier length​(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the length of the actual value
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • trim

      StringVerifier trim()
      Trims whitespace at the beginning and end of the actual value.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier

    • isTrimmed

      StringVerifier isTrimmed()
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing whitespace.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value is not trimmed
      See Also:
      trim()

    • asInetAddress

      InetAddressVerifier asInetAddress()
      Ensures that the actual value contains a valid Internet address format.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the Internet address representation of the value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value does not contain a valid Internet address format

    • asInetAddress

      StringVerifier asInetAddress​(Consumer<InetAddressVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asInetAddress() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies Internet addresses
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asUri

      UriVerifier asUri()
      Returns a verifier for the URI representation of the value.

      Technically-speaking, there is no such thing as an invalid URI format. Per RFC3986, any String can be represented as a relative URI, but Java's implementation is based on an older specification where this was not the case.

      Returns:
      a verifier for the URI representation of the value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value cannot be converted to a URI
      See Also:
      Discussion of Java URI vs RFC3986

    • asUri

      StringVerifier asUri​(Consumer<UriVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asUri() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies URIs
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asUrl

      UrlVerifier asUrl()
      Returns a verifier for the URL representation of the value.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the URL representation of the value
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the actual value cannot be converted to a URL

    • asUrl

      StringVerifier asUrl​(Consumer<UrlVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asUrl() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies URLs
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null
